g.text: STRATEGY B: Asymmetric Distance Computation (O(M) Complexity)

ol.li: Memory Traffic: Naive strategy moves 3,072 Bytes (vector) + 3,072 Bytes (query) per record. ADC moves only 8 Bytes (codes).
CPU Throughput: Naive requires full L2 distance logic on reconstructed floats. ADC performs simple table lookups and scalar additions.
Cache Locality: LUT (8x256 floats = 8KB) fits entirely in L1 Data Cache, eliminating DRAM bottlenecks found in Strategy A.

g.text: d(q₀, C₀₀) | d(q₀, C₀₁) | ... | d(q₀, C₀₂₅₅)
d(q₁, C₁₀) | d(q₁, C₁₁) | ... | d(q₁, C₁₂₅₅)
